_meta.description: GeoWorld Framework — Scientifically peer-reviewed parameter database. Each parameter adjustment is annotated with a [REF] tag and quantitative justification.
changelog: v2.2: Full academic review. Adjustments in carbon_price (PRT, CHN), capacity_factor (CHN solar, PRT wind), capacity_density wind (alignment with McKenna 2020), lifecycle EF (solar, hydro), upstream EF (gas). Added rigorous _review_notes per parameter.
v2.1: Added hierarchical fallback logic and unit normalization.
v2.0: Complete structural refactoring from legacy parameters.xlsx format.

units.owa_weights: 3 weights summing to 1.0 (descending order: best to worst criterion)

key_references.[IPCC2022]: IPCC AR6 WG3 (2022). Climate Change 2022: Mitigation. doi:10.1017/9781009157926. Lifecycle GHG (Annex II Table A.II.4)
key_references.[IEA-LCEF2024]: IEA Life Cycle Upstream Emission Factors 2024.
key_references.[IRENA2024c]: IRENA (2024). Renewable Power Generation Costs in 2023. ISBN 978-92-9260-619-3
key_references.[IRENA2025c]: IRENA (2025). Renewable Power Generation Costs in 2024.
key_references.[WB2024]: World Bank (2024). State and Trends of Carbon Pricing 2024. DOI:10.1596/978-1-4648-2127-1
key_references.[Bolinger2022]: Bolinger & Bolinger (2022). IEEE J. Photovoltaics 12(1):99-113. doi:10.1109/JPHOTOV.2021.3130574
key_references.[Enevoldsen2021]: Enevoldsen & Jacobson (2021). Energy Sustain. Dev. 60:40-51. doi:10.1016/j.esd.2020.11.004
key_references.[McKenna2020]: McKenna et al. (2020). Energy Policy 147:111693. doi:10.1016/j.enpol.2020.111693
key_references.[Hwang1981]: Hwang & Yoon (1981). Multiple Attribute Decision Making. Springer. doi:10.1007/978-3-642-48318-9
key_references.[ESA-WC2021]: Zanaga et al. (2021). ESA WorldCover 10m 2020. Zenodo. doi:10.5281/zenodo.5571936
key_references.[IRENA2023flex]: IRENA (2023). Power System Flexibility for the Energy Transition.
key_references.[EC-ETS2026]: European Commission (2026). Trends in Carbon Intensity and EU ETS.

abatement_defaults._review_notes: Global defaults reviewed against IPCC AR6 WG3 (lifecycle) and IEA EF 2024 (operational). Important distinction: thermal_emission_factors are OPERATIONAL (direct combustion) while renewable_lifecycle are FULL LIFECYCLE.

default._carbon_price_note: [WB2024][IPCC2022] 75 USD/tCO2e is used as a policy-shadow price consistent with least-cost 1.5°C pathways by 2030 (IPCC AR6 WG3 Ch.13, range 63–127 USD/tCO2e at 5% discount rate). This is NOT the observed market median: the World Bank (2024) reports coverage-weighted average below 15 USD/tCO2e across active instruments globally. The 75 USD figure aligns with IEA NZE 2050 advanced economy carbon price trajectory (IEA WEO 2024, Table A.8).
default.carbon_price_source: IPCC AR6 WG3 Ch.13 Table 13.SM.1; IEA WEO 2024 Announced Pledges Scenario; World Bank STCP 2024 (market context only)
default._penetration_note: [IRENA2023flex] Technical limit without significant grid storage typically ranges from 40-60% in mixed grids. 0.60 is set as the upper bound without substantial BESS/Pumped Hydro.
default._lcoe_threshold_note: [IRENA2024c] Global average LCOE for solar = 44 USD/MWh; onshore wind = 33 USD/MWh. A 60 USD/MWh threshold is appropriately conservative for comparison with gas SRMC (40-65 USD/MWh).

thermal_emission_factors_tco2e_gwh._source: [IPCC2022] AR6 WG3 Annex II Table A.II.4 — harmonized lifecycle medians. Unit: tCO2e/GWh = gCO2eq/kWh.
thermal_emission_factors_tco2e_gwh._note: These are full lifecycle factors. For direct combustion only, refer to IEA EF 2024.
thermal_emission_factors_tco2e_gwh._gas_note: [IPCC2022] Median: 490 gCO2eq/kWh; range: 410-650. Includes upstream methane leakage.
thermal_emission_factors_tco2e_gwh._oil_note: [IPCC2022] Fuel-oil/residues: ~650-820 gCO2eq/kWh. 750 is a robust median.

thermal_upstream_emissions_gco2_kwh._source: [IEA-LCEF2024] IEA Life Cycle Upstream Emission Factors 2024.
thermal_upstream_emissions_gco2_kwh._gas_upstream_note: [IEA-LCEF2024] Upstream gas: 10-25 gCO2/kWh_el depending on origin (LNG vs pipeline).

thermal_cf._source: [IRENA2024c] + IEA WEO 2024 — Capacity Factors by plant type.
thermal_cf._gas_cf_note: Global average CCGT CF 2023: 40-55% (IEA). 0.45 reflects mixed base/peak usage.
thermal_cf._oil_cf_note: Fuel-oil plants primarily serve as peaking/emergency backup. 0.35 acts as an upper-bound technical availability metric.

thermal_marginal_cost._source: IEA WEO 2024 — Short-Run Marginal Costs by technology (fuel + O&M; excluding carbon).
thermal_marginal_cost._gas_mc_note: SRMC CCGT 2023: 35-65 USD/MWh. 48 USD/MWh is a robust median for non-European markets.
thermal_marginal_cost._oil_mc_note: SRMC fuel-oil: 70-130 USD/MWh based on crude prices and thermal efficiency (<40%).

renewable_lifecycle_gco2_kwh._source: [IPCC2022] AR6 WG3 Annex II Table A.II.4.
renewable_lifecycle_gco2_kwh._solar_lc_note: [IPCC2022] Harmonized median utility-scale c-Si: 48 gCO2eq/kWh. Recent literature notes a decline to 30-40 gCO2eq/kWh due to cleaner manufacturing mixes; 48 retained for conservatism.
renewable_lifecycle_gco2_kwh._biomass_lc_note: [IPCC2022] Median solid biomass: 230 gCO2eq/kWh. Highly dependent on feedstock type and ILUC impacts.

EGY.name: Egypt
EGY._grid_mix_note: Natural gas accounts for 80-85% of total electricity generation (IRENA, 2023).

solar._pd_note: [Bolinger2022] Egypt's flat desert expanses are highly optimized for dense, fixed-tilt and tracking arrays.

wind._cf_note: [IRENA2024c] The Gulf of Suez region demonstrates exceptional wind CFs (0.33-0.40).

EGY._slope_note: 5 degrees limits site selection to vast planar desert tracts, maximizing constructability.

thermal_capacity_mw._gas_note: Reflects highly efficient CCGT fleet; total installed gas capacity including legacy plants is higher (NREA, 2024).

abatement._cp_note: [WB2024] Modeled as a shadow carbon price based on the IEA WEO 2024 developing economies proxy.
abatement.carbon_price_source: Shadow carbon price — IEA WEO 2024 developing economies proxy
abatement._pf_note: [IRENA2023flex] A gas-dominated grid offers moderate flexibility, but lacks the buffering capacity of hydro. Limited to 0.50 without storage scaling.

net_zero_target._ndc_note: [EGY-NDC2022] Target specifies a conditional 33% reduction vs BAU trajectory in 2030, not an absolute reduction from the base year.
net_zero_target.source: Egypt Updated NDC (2022). Ministry of Environment.

lcoe._note: Egypt achieves highly competitive baseline LCOEs due to premier resource quality (e.g., Benban, Gulf of Suez). WACC is elevated primarily by macroeconomic and currency devaluation risks.

biomass._opex_note: Feedstock relies heavily on Nile Delta agricultural residues (e.g., rice straw).

AUS.name: Australia
AUS._grid_mix_note: Coal accounts for 45-50% of generation in the NEM; renewables exceed 35% and are rising rapidly (AEMO, 2024).

solar._luf_note: [Bolinger2022] Australia's exceptionally low population density supports a higher theoretical land utilization factor (20% vs standard 5%) for macro-potential mapping.
solar._threshold_note: A highly stringent threshold of 0.70 isolates the absolute optimal spatial domains, aligning with mega-project export models (e.g., SunCable).
solar._cf_note: [IRENA2024c] Australia solar CF averages 0.22-0.28 depending heavily on latitudinal displacement.

wind._cd_note: [McKenna2020] Modeled at the upper boundary (7.5 MW/km2) to reflect ultra-large-scale remote installations using >3MW modern turbines.
wind._threshold_note: A highly permissive threshold of 0.25 is utilized to capture maximum theoretical wind boundaries, exposing 75% of suboptimal yet technically viable area.

biomass._threshold_note: Permissive threshold aligned with the wind methodology to establish an absolute theoretical resource baseline.

thermal_capacity_mw._coal_note: Capacity in rapid decline via scheduled decommissioning timelines.
thermal_capacity_mw._oil_note: Significant diesel generation persists in remote and off-grid mining communities.

abatement._cp_note: [WB2024, CER2024] Approximates the Australian Carbon Credit Unit (ACCU) price. The 2023 Safeguard Mechanism reform acts as a de facto carbon price.
abatement.carbon_price_source: Australia ACCU Scheme + Safeguard Mechanism — CER (2024)
abatement._pf_note: [IRENA2023flex] High VRE penetration causes increasing localized curtailment in states like SA, maintaining the uncurtailed limit near 0.50 without broader transmission/BESS upgrades.

net_zero_target._ndc_note: [AUS-CCA2022] Legislated by the Climate Change Act 2022.
net_zero_target.source: Australia Climate Change Act 2022. Commonwealth of Australia.

lcoe._note: Australia features a mature renewable sector utilizing CfD auctions. Elevated logistics and domestic labor costs are largely offset by supreme generation yields.

wind._capex_note: [IRENA2024c] Australia onshore wind 2023: 1450-1650 USD/kW. Elevated by heavy civil works in remote grid corridors.

biomass._opex_note: Transport distances in remote areas add substantial logistics costs to agricultural residue feedstock.

IND.name: India
IND._grid_mix_note: Coal generation remains highly dominant at ~71% in 2023 (CEA, 2024).

solar._pd_note: [IRENA2024c, MNRE2024] Standard tracking configurations in premier states (Rajasthan, Gujarat) support 45-55 MW_AC/km2.
solar._weights_note: DNI is relevant in the northwest for potential CSP mapping, while GHI dominates the subcontinent.
solar._cf_note: [IRENA2024c] Averages highly productive northern deserts against monsoon-heavy southern belts.

wind._cd_note: [MNRE2024, Enevoldsen2021] Indian wind farms exhibit moderate spatial density; 6.0 MW/km2 is a robust baseline.
wind._cf_note: [IRENA2024c] National median balancing strong Tamil Nadu yields with moderate inland speeds.

biomass._cf_note: [IRENA2024c] Biomass plants utilizing crop residue and bagasse typically run as reliable baseload.

IND._slope_note: Accommodates the expansive Deccan Plateau while effectively excluding complex Himalayan topography.

thermal_capacity_mw._source: CEA India (2024). Annual Report on Generation 2023-24.
thermal_capacity_mw._coal_note: Coal capacity approximates 210 GW and remains the backbone of the grid.
thermal_capacity_mw._gas_note: Gas fleet is frequently underutilized due to domestic feedstock shortages.

abatement._cp_note: [WB2024] Modeled proxy based on India's indirect Coal Cess framework; explicit ETS is not yet operational.
abatement.carbon_price_source: Indirect proxy — India Coal Cess / Clean Energy Cess.
abatement._pf_note: [IRENA2023flex] Heavy reliance on inflexible coal limits VRE absorption ceilings without massive grid overhauls.

net_zero_target._ndc_note: [IND-NDC2022] Target specifies a 45% reduction in emissions intensity per unit of GDP, not absolute reductions.
net_zero_target.source: India Updated NDC (2022). MoEFCC. Net Zero 2070 (COP26).

lcoe._note: India demonstrates highly competitive capital costs bolstered by aggressive localization schemes (e.g., PLI). Financing costs remain elevated compared to East Asia.

solar._capex_note: [IRENA2024c] Utility-scale solar median ~600 USD/kW, driven down by SECI reverse auctions.
solar._wacc_note: [BloombergNEF2024] A 9% discount rate captures persistent grid reliability and off-taker risk profiles.

wind._capex_note: [IRENA2024c] Robust domestic supply chains (e.g., Suzlon) yield highly competitive onshore wind CAPEX.

biomass._capex_note: [IRENA2024c] High reliance on rice/wheat straw processing and sugarcane.
biomass._opex_note: Variable depending on regional availability; states like Punjab exhibit lower feedstock marginal costs.

ETH.name: Ethiopia
ETH._grid_mix_note: Grid is near 100% renewable, anchored overwhelmingly by massive hydrological reserves (EEP, 2024).

solar._pd_note: [IRENA2024c, ESMAP2022] The central highlands and Rift Valley support excellent utility-scale spatial density.
solar._cf_note: [ESMAP2022, IRENA2024c] Yields are robust across high-altitude plateaus.

wind._cf_note: [IRENA2024c, EEP2024] Highlands and escarpment geography generate exceptional localized wind corridors.

ETH._slope_note: A higher 15-degree tolerance is necessary to unlock potential across the predominantly mountainous Ethiopian topography.

thermal_capacity_mw._gas_note: Extremely limited utility, utilized strictly for peaking or localized stabilization.

abatement._pf_note: [IRENA2023flex] Massive reservoir hydro systems (e.g., GERD) offer extraordinary baseload buffering, allowing near-frictionless VRE integration.

net_zero_target._ndc_note: [ETH-NDC2021] Conditional upon international financing; models a 68.8% reduction against projected BAU trajectories.
net_zero_target._nzy_note: Aims to achieve a universal clean energy system by 2030.
net_zero_target.source: Ethiopia Updated NDC (2021). Ministry of Finance.

lcoe._note: Macroeconomic friction, foreign exchange scarcity, and inland transport severely elevate baseline costs, though partially mitigated by concessional development bank financing.

solar._capex_note: [IRENA2024c] Sub-Saharan average adjusted upwards to model landlocked supply chain premiums.
solar._wacc_note: [WB-PPIAF] Simulates strict sovereign risk profiling; assumes limited access to purely commercial finance.

wind._capex_note: Complex logistics involved in transporting supersized turbines into the highlands generates structural CAPEX inflation.

biomass._capex_note: Assumes optimization around existing agricultural processing (e.g., coffee, sugar).

IRN.name: Iran
IRN._grid_mix_note: Gas accounts for ~67% of generation; hydro ~11%; negligible coal (MOE, 2024).

solar._pd_note: [IRENA2024c] High altitude arid plateaus represent optimal domains for standard tracking arrays.
solar._weights_note: Intense clear-sky conditions heavily favor DNI weighting, maximizing CSP models.
solar._cf_note: [IRENA2024c] National yields are bolstered by cloudless highland geometries.

wind._cf_note: [IRENA2024c] Regionalized corridors (e.g., Manjil) provide stable, high-yield generation profiles.

biomass._yield_note: Predominantly arid terrain sharply limits biomass to northern agricultural pockets.

thermal_capacity_mw._coal_note: Coal maintains a statistically negligible presence in the thermal fleet.
thermal_capacity_mw._gas_note: Massive domestic gas reserves underwrite the vast majority of grid operations.

abatement._cp_note: [WB2024] International isolation precludes integration into standardized carbon markets.
abatement._pf_note: [IRENA2023flex] A gas-dominant profile allows moderate dispatch flexibility to offset VRE intermittency.

net_zero_target._ndc_note: [IRN-INDC2015] Initial INDC proposes an unconditional 4% reduction against BAU, remaining unratified and un-updated.
net_zero_target.source: Iran INDC (2015). No updated framework submitted.

lcoe._note: Capital flows and technology procurement are highly restricted by international sanctions, generating a unique, isolated risk and cost environment.

solar._capex_note: Derived via regional comparators adjusted mathematically for extreme import constraints.
solar._wacc_note: Models extreme localized financing hurdles stripped of international debt markets.

ARE.name: United Arab Emirates
ARE._grid_mix_note: Gas constitutes ~76% of generation; nuclear ~14%; solar ~9% (ADWEC/DEWA, 2024).

solar._pd_note: [IRENA2024c] Uninterrupted planar topographies and extreme irradiance accommodate ultra-dense bifacial tracking systems.
solar._weights_note: Hyper-arid conditions maximize DNI, favoring technologies reliant on direct insolation.
solar._cf_note: [IRENA2024c, DEWA2024] Operating complexes report world-leading utilization rates.

wind._cd_note: [IRENA2024c] Resource mapping enforces a conservative density due to structurally limited inland kinetic profiles.
wind._thr_note: Higher threshold mathematically confines analysis to scarce high-yield coastal strips.
wind._cf_note: [IRENA2024c] Conservative estimation aligns with measured coastal averages.

biomass._yield_note: Arid climate yields highly constrained biomass potential, limited primarily to agricultural residues and MSW.

ARE._slope_note: Aggressive slope restriction isolates massive planar desert basins while strictly excluding mountainous northern perimeters.

thermal_capacity_mw._coal_note: No active coal capacity; strategic emphasis favors zero-carbon deployment.
thermal_capacity_mw._gas_note: Gas remains the foundational balancing instrument of the power grid.
thermal_capacity_mw._oil_note: Maintained primarily for localized peaking and system security.

abatement._cp_note: [WB2024] No functional formal carbon market mechanics exist in the baseline state.
abatement._pf_note: [IRENA2023flex] Highly responsive modern CCGT fleets permit robust VRE integration thresholds.

net_zero_target._base_note: The UAE NDC architecture refrains from establishing an absolute historical baseline.
net_zero_target._ndc_note: [ARE-NDC2023] Target defines a relative reduction measured exclusively against forward-looking BAU scenarios.
net_zero_target._nzy_note: [ARE-NZ2050] Energy sector transition targets advanced milestones ahead of general economic neutrality.
net_zero_target.source: UAE NDC (2023 Update); UAE Net Zero 2050 Strategic Initiative (2021).

lcoe._note: The UAE achieves absolute minimum global utility-scale solar LCOE through massive scale, free land provisions, and sovereign-backed financial structuring.

solar._capex_note: [IRENA2024c] Mega-projects (e.g., Al Dhafra) report record-breaking installation efficiencies.
solar._wacc_note: Discount rates are heavily suppressed by sovereign wealth capital and strict currency pegs to the USD.

wind._capex_note: Constrained scale relative to solar maintains standard global CAPEX profiles.

biomass._capex_note: Focuses heavily on bespoke Waste-to-Energy (WtE) installations.

SAU.name: Saudi Arabia
SAU._grid_mix_note: Gas generates ~59%; direct oil combustion ~36%; renewables scaling rapidly via NEOM (SEC, 2024).

solar._pd_note: [IRENA2024c] Hyper-arid plains support maximum density footprints aligned with national deployment mandates.
solar._weights_note: Extreme DNI resource commands prioritized weighting for robust CSP models.
solar._cf_note: [IRENA2024c] Modeled equivalent to leading regional utility installations.

wind._cf_note: [IRENA2024c] Confined to highly specific coastal and high-plateau topographies.

biomass._yield_note: Absolute scarcity of biomass resource aside from hyper-localized municipal streams.

SAU._slope_note: Configured to explicitly target the vast, flat Rub' al Khali geometries.

thermal_capacity_mw._gas_note: Represents the backbone of dispatchable capacity.
thermal_capacity_mw._oil_note: Massive legacy reliance on direct crude combustion remains.

abatement._cp_note: [WB2024] Fossil-fuel subsidies remain structurally dominant over pricing deterrents.
abatement._pf_note: [IRENA2023flex] Accommodates the highly dispatchable nature of the fossil fleet, providing massive balancing headroom.

net_zero_target._base_note: No absolute base-year specified in the national framework.
net_zero_target._ndc_note: [SAU-NDC2021] Architecture dictates absolute avoidance targets (278 MtCO2eq/yr) over relative baselines.
net_zero_target._nzy_note: [SAU-NZ2060] Saudi Green Initiative (COP26) formalized the 2060 timeline.
net_zero_target.source: Saudi Arabia NDC (2021); Saudi Green Initiative (2021).

lcoe._note: Vision 2030 mandates generate historically low LCOEs supported by immense sovereign scale.

solar._capex_note: [IRENA2024c] Utility-scale builds report extreme efficiency and suppressed BOS costs.

wind._capex_note: Aligned with isolated major builds (e.g., Dumat Al Jandal).

biomass._capex_note: Restricted purely to municipal scale MSW logic.

fallback_logic.description: Hierarchical fallback system for undefined national parameters.
fallback_logic._methodology_note: Priority cascade: country_specific > region_default > income_level_default > grid_mix_default > global_average. Categorizations map directly to World Bank and IEA definitions.

region_defaults._source: [WB2024] Regional carbon pricing medians; [IRENA2024c] regional capacity factors.

grid_mix_defaults._source: [IRENA2023flex, IRENA2024c] Intensity and flexibility bounds by baseload dominance.

global_average._source: [IPCC2022] Lifecycle medians; [WB2024] carbon price global average.
global_average._cp_note: [WB2024] Global weighted average of operational instruments. Acts as an aspirational proxy reflecting low-cost marginal mitigation dynamics.

thermal_ef_api._note: Primary source updated to IEA Emission Factors 2024. API authentication required.

carbon_price_api._note: Public API. Updated annually in Q2. Filters applied to separate ETS from carbon tax frameworks.

net_zero_api._note: Climate Watch API serves as the primary structural interface due to CAT API instability.

iea_emissions_api._note: Accesses the 'CO2 Highlights' repository for absolute historical baselines.

calculation_parameters.merit_order_rule: Emissions-optimal merit order substitution: thermal fleets with the highest marginal emission factors are substituted first.
calculation_parameters._mac_note: Marginal Abatement Cost (USD/tCO2e). Methodology aligns with McKinsey Abatement Cost Curves (2009) and Vogt-Schilb & Hallegatte (2017).
calculation_parameters.scope_calculation: Follows GHG Protocol Corporate Standard (2015) and Scope 2 Guidance. Includes Scope 3 Category 3 upstream fuel emissions.
calculation_parameters.penetration_limit_note: IRENA bounds technical uncurtailed VRE integration limits relative to systemic dispatchable flexibility.

10._solar_note: Hard exclusion due to unacceptable biodiversity impacts and severe physical shading.
10._wind_note: Possible but heavily penalized by aerodynamic turbulence and logistical clearing costs.
10._biomass_note: Strictly excludes primary logging. Permissible only under highly controlled sustainable residue frameworks.

40._solar_note: Scores depressed to reflect food-energy nexus competition. Viable exclusively for advanced agrivoltaic modeling.
40._wind_note: Minimal surface footprint makes onshore wind highly compatible with concurrent agricultural operations.
40._biomass_note: Optimal domain for agricultural residue collection and dedicated energy crop cultivation.

50._solar_note: Zero suitability for utility-scale footprints. Rooftop potential requires a separate methodological model.

60._solar_note: Absolute maximum suitability. Optimal deployment domain.

80._solar_note: Zero suitability. Floating solar (FPV) requires a discrete spatial model.
80._wind_note: Zero suitability. Offshore wind constitutes a fundamentally distinct framework.
